confidence_justification_reported: The budget-neutrality arithmetic is robust because the key fiscal and enterprise-base figures come from current official Polish sources, but the legal assessment remains design-dependent at the Article 401 VAT boundary.
key_data_limitations_reported: No single official 2025 dataset provides a perfectly universal economy-wide turnover base matching the legal concept of a hypothetical turnover tax, so the base had to be proxied from GUS enterprise-revenue data.
Micro-enterprise turnover for 2025 was not available in the same current official form as the 2025 data for larger firms, so the analysis used the latest 2024 micro data and scaling assumptions.

Financial institutions, insurers, and some special institutional sectors are only partially covered or excluded in the main enterprise datasets, while turnover is conceptually difficult for those sectors anyway.
The estimate is static: it assumes no behavioural changes, no base erosion from evasion or restructuring, and no second-round effects on prices, wages, or firm exit.
